title: clausena anisum-olens subsp. calciphila
intro: Clausena anisum-olens subsp. calciphila, also known as the Anise-Leafed Clausena or Anise-Scented Clausena, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Clausena anisum-olens subsp. calciphila include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its health benefits.
